content.answer:
Hi

Please find the attached memo for tomorrow's discussion.
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1RgQBtoEGj9N55IFLK5Tdw85CpthY0f9YQ5-aZFmOlPk/edit?usp=sharing

Thanks
Leo

Hi Terence

Please find the below as follow ups from the meeting.
[image: image.png]

Thanks
Leo

Hi Terence

To update on item #1 and #2, please find the below summary (detailed study
is attached).

*#1. GMS auto toggle-on in SG/MY/TH/VN*

   - Remediation plan: suspend GMS auto toggle-on for new shops going
   forwards (mass toggle-on for all shops have been performed in Feb)
      - 2026FY impact is estimated to be -*$553k for EBITDA *(*-0.01pp for
      CB* take rate, *-0.001pp for platform* take rate for SG/MY/TH/VN)
      - *We will mitigate this potential loss and deliver as budgeted* with
      seller management and product measures.

*#2. Auto escrow 2% with default enrollment in PH*

   - *Remediation plan **A*: shift to opt-in mode
      - The estimated impact for FY2026 is *-$1.3m for EBITDA *(*-0.06pp
      for CB* take rate, *-0.01pp for platform* take rate for PH)
   - *Remediation plan B* (Recommended): transition to 5% fee-based model
   (like other countries) from Oct, when regulatory environment and seller
   sentiment cool down
      - The estimated impact for FY2026 is *+$8m for EBITDA *(*+0.36pp for
      CB* take rate, *+0.06pp for platform* take rate for PH)
   - We are currently in discussion with PH on the remediation plan decision


Best
Leo

Hi Ian,


Sorry for the late reply as I was sick last few days. CN side aligned with
us on the take rate impact beforehand, deck wise not. Pls find replies as
follows.


How much are we supposed to charge, how much was utilized, how much not
utilized, how bad is the utilization rate and is there a particular
concentration?

~87-90% was utilized*. The key gap is with 3P SLS sellers, driven not by
GMV Max auto-on, but by sellers’ inability to spend large ad top up.
Evidences showed in below table,

   -

    FBS/3PF (more short-tail) utilization rate changed little across the
   Phase 1/2/3
   -

   3P SLS utilization fell post-rollout in both Phase 2 and 3

I suggest we monitor 2-3 more weeks of top up to ensure the judgement,
current phase 3 only got 1 week

Table: CNCB Ads Expenditure/Ads Topup% by period

[image: image.png]

*Note: Ads Utilization uses the same period for spend and top-ups, which
can look inflated if auto top-ups accumulated from different period vs
expenditure, however, the rolling view is more representative when more
weeks data in.


how does this impact the TR% and how will we get sellers to utilize the ads?

even with GMV Max auto-off for new sellers, CN shared results is aligned
with us

   -

   Last TP meeting: after the meeting, we mentioned that the uplift was
   1.3% vs 1.8% committed (~0.5% short) due to lower ad top-ups during CNY
   -

   Latest week (ad credits and GMV both normal time): take rate uplift vs
   pre-compulsory period is on track 1.9% vs 1.8% committed (see table below)

I suggest we monitor 2-3 more weeks of top up to ensure the judgement, current
phase 3 only got 1 week


   -

   #1 How to solve “top-ups made but not auto spend”?  (this email key
   focus: CN Regulation prohibit Deemed Consent)


   -

   1) from seller mgmt wise, a) educate on ROAS protection and GMV Max
   algorithms to address concerns about GMV Max on, b) incentive sellers to
   st

content.question: Ok so we are 0.1% better than committed?

That is probably ok then..



*From:* Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Tuesday, 24 March 2026 8:47 am
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>
*Cc:* Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: [Pre-read] Changes to CN eCommerce Regulations



Hi Ian,



Sorry for the late reply as I was sick last few days. CN side aligned with
us on the take rate impact beforehand, deck wise not. Pls find replies as
follows.



How much are we supposed to charge, how much was utilized, how much not
utilized, how bad is the utilization rate and is there a particular
concentration?

*>> Suppose to charge at 5%+VAT of charge base, and out of total top-up,
~87-90% was utilized*. The key gap is with 3P SLS sellers, driven not by
GMV Max auto-on, but by sellers’ inability to spend large ad top up.
Evidences showed in below table,*

   - * FBS/3PF (more short-tail) utilization rate changed little across the
   Phase 1/2/3*
   - *3P SLS utilization fell post-rollout in both Phase 2 and 3*

*I suggest we monitor 2-3 more weeks of top up to ensure the judgement,
current phase 3 only got 1 week*

*Table: CNCB Ads Expenditure/Ads Topup% by period*

**Note: Ads Utilization uses the same period for spend and top-ups, which
can look inflated if auto top-ups accumulated from different period vs
expenditure, however, the rolling view is more representative when more
weeks data in.*



how does this impact the TR% and how will we get sellers to utilize the ads?

*>>About impact to Takerate: Based on recent week, targets look achievable
even with GMV Max auto-off for new sellers, CN shared results is aligned
with us*

   - *Last TP meeting: after the meeting, we mentioned that the uplift was
   1.3% vs 1.8% committed (~0.5% short) due to lower ad top-ups during CNY*
   - *Latest week (ad credits and GMV both normal time): take rate uplift
   vs pre-compulsory period is on track 1.9% vs 1.8% committed (see table
   below) *

*I suggest we monitor 2-3 more weeks of top up to ensure the judgement,
current phase 3 only got 1 week*

*>>About get sellers to utilize the ads: split into two parts to answer*

   - *#1 How to solve “top-ups made but not auto spend”?
